alspac.value: The Avon Longitudinal Study of Parents and Children (ALSPAC) is a transgenerational prospective observational study investigating influences on health and development across the life course. It considers multiple genetic
social and other environmental exposures in relation to a similarly diverse range of health
The Avon Longitudinal Study of Parents and Children (ALSPAC) is a transgenerational prospective observational study investigating influences on health and development across the life course.
United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland (the)
Pregnant women residents in the three Health Districts of the old administrative county of Avon
Estimated delivery date between 1 April 1991 and 31 December 1992
Pregnant women originally resident in Avon but migrating out of the catchment area prior to delivery unless they had completed the questionnaire scheduled for the third trimester of pregnancy
ONS (deaths and cancer registrations); National Pupil Database; General Practice Research Database

The vast majority of requests are approved as the system only checks availability of data and whether a cost will need to be charged (in most cases <£1000) to cover the staff resources required to put together a data set for collaborators.
the Wellcome Trust and the University of Bristol currently provide core funding support for ALSPAC (WT092731). Data collection is funded from a wide range of sources

which are detailed on the ALSPAC website: http://www.bristol.ac.uk/alspac/participants/ethics/funding/. AB is funded by the Wellcome Trust (WT086118/Z/08/Z) and the MRC/Wellcome Trust strategic support. AF is funded by an MRC Research Fellowship (G0701594). DAL
AF and GDS work in a Centre that receives funding from the MRC (G0600705).
We are extremely grateful to all the families who took part in this study
the midwives for their help in recruiting them

concrete.value: The CONCRETE study aims to determine whether direct GP access to CT CCS leads to earlier and more cost-effective diagnosis and treatment of CAD in patients with atypical AP or non-specific thoracic complaints
CONCRETE is a pragmatic multicentre study with a cluster-randomised design. This type of design is increasingly used in primary care for the evaluation of healthcare interventions.

lifelinenext.value: Lifelines NEXT is a prospective birth cohort designed to study the effects of intrinsic and extrinsic determinants on health and disease in a four-generation design
Lifelines NEXT is an observational prospective birth cohort with a transgenerational design of up to four generations.
Dutch Perinatal Data Register; Perined; electronic files of the regional youth and family centers

the provinces of Friesland and Groningen and the municipality of Groningen. Furthermore Philips provided continuous measurement devices for this add-on initiative. fathers and children in Lifelines NEXT. We also thank the whole Lifelines NEXT team for their ongoing effort to build this important cohort. Furthermore we also want to thank all maternity care providers for their efforts to recruit participants and collect materials during childbirth. We thank Kate McIntyre for carefully reading the manuscript.

The intervention arm of the LOFIT study involves patients receiving guidance on lifestyle management in collaboration with community-based lifestyle initiatives compared to usual care.
The usual care arm of the LOFIT study involves patients receiving usual care without additional lifestyle management guidance from community-based initiatives.

test_zelfhy.value: A pragmatic randomised controlled trial designed to determine the (cost) effectiveness of home-based guided hypnotherapy plus care as usual (CAU) compared with CAU alone for children with functional abdominal pain (FAP) or irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) in primary care.
A pragmatic randomised controlled trial designed to determine the (cost) effectiveness of home-based guided hypnotherapy plus CAU compared with CAU alone for children with FAP or IBS in primary care.
